arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's upbeat Middlesbrough message as they look to bounce back against Hull City
arts.sum: "It’s a quick turnaround, and we’ll just have to pick the bodies that are fit and ready for Saturday. Looking ahead to the Hull game, he said: "There was disappointment after the game, and frustration at the goal as well.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough sweating on key men as they prepare for big Hull City clash
arts.sum: Middlesbrough head coach Michael Carrick admits he faces a nervous 48 hours ahead of Saturday's clash with managerless Hull City with a flurry of players unwell. “We’ve got a good squad and good players, so it’s not an excuse.

arts.ttl: Ben Doak needs help from Middlesbrough team-mates as Liverpool loan star gets 'natural' treatment
arts.sum: Michael Carrick says it's natural that teams are starting to target Middlesbrough star Ben Doak, but he's confident his side can continue to share the load and help the Liverpool loanee continue to thrive. It meant the lively wide man was unable to have his usual impact in the game, even if he still managed to come closest to scoring for Boro when he smashed the post late on.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough transfer failed after Boro ‘invented problem he never had’ – Player unhappy with club
arts.sum: Yaya Sanogo has told So Foot he came close to joining Middlesbrough earlier in his career. The Frenchman spent four years at Arsenal and struggled to make an impact at the north London club. They sent him out on loan before releasing him at the end of the 2016/17 season. arts.ttl: Michael Dawson's conversation with brother Andy ahead of Middlesbrough trip for Hull interim boss
arts.sum: Former England international Michael Dawson admits it's going to be 'hard' for his brother to lead Hull City at Middlesbrough on Saturday, with Andy Dawson placed in interim charge following the sacking of Tim Walter. Andy will take charge of the Tigers on Saturday as they look to arrest a slump in form that's seen them lose their last four games, with no wins in their last nine.

arts.ttl: 'We're not in a good spot' - Hull City skipper makes stark admission about Tigers - Hull Live
arts.sum: Lewie Coyle admits Hull City are not in a good spot but says he will do his best to help lift the players for Saturday's trip to Middlesbrough. People in general are a lot quieter than maybe I'm used to but I'll get around the boys and see if they need anything.
